response.justification: Bogdan has a genuine geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map, and distributed-systems exposure from high-throughput work at Dunnhumby, but these are peripheral to the role's core demands. The position is fundamentally a C++ or Rust systems engineering role focused on routing tile pipelines, and the candidate's entire career trajectory is in TypeScript, JavaScript, and Node.js. His C++ experience is isolated to a single six-month role in 2014–2015 (NI LabWindows) and a bachelor thesis project — neither in production routing nor at meaningful scale. There is no Rust experience, no routing or navigation data pipeline experience, and no evidence of geospatial work beyond web-based GIS tools. The skills gap is too wide to bridge through on-the-job growth for a mid-senior hire.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map open-source projects
Distributed systems at production scale — low-latency, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
Strong quality culture: consistent application of TDD, trunk-based development, and CI/CD across multiple roles
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience — primary language requirement is essentially unmet
No routing data pipeline, routing tile, or navigation guidance system experience of any kind
Career has been almost exclusively TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js web and backend work, making a switch to systems-level C++ a very large leap for a senior hire
